steps.title: State the two findings
steps.content: The passage gives a contrast: a greater share of Quito respondents reported using parks, 82.9%, than Lima respondents, 72.7%. But Quito respondents also reported much lower access to desired amenities near parks than Lima respondents did.

steps.title: Use the author's conclusion
steps.content: Because Quito has higher park use despite lower reported access to nearby desired amenities, the park-use difference cannot be explained by Quito residents having more access to those amenities. The main idea must keep that causal claim narrow.

steps.title: Avoid invented causes
steps.content: Choice A invents a claim about overall amenities in Quito and Lima. Choice B predicts what would increase park use, which the passage does not test. Choice C invents a claim about Quito having more parks. Choice D restates the passage's conclusion: higher park use in Quito is not due to greater access to amenities near parks. The answer is D.
